Sentence: File operations are standardized system calls enabling core file management: create allocates new space and adds a directory entry after checking availability; open searches the directory, loads the FCB into the open-file table, and returns a descriptor for quick access; read fetches data from the current file pointer position (with separate read/write pointers for efficiency); write appends or overwrites at the pointer, often updating metadata; close flushes changes and removes the entry from open-file tables; seek (reposition) adjusts the pointer without data transfer; delete frees space and erases the directory entry; truncate shortens the file while preserving attributes; and append adds to the end Entity1: System Calls (TOPIC). Entity2: Directory (CONCEPT).
